item.title: Examining circuit boards from the Space Shuttle's I/O Processor 摘要
item.description:
The Space Shuttle's I/O Processor (IOP) was a critical component linking the main CPU to the Shuttle's systems via 24 high-speed networks. It featured an unusual multi-threaded architecture with 25 virtual processors and two instruction sets. The IOP used network interface cards called MIA (Multiplexer Interface Adapter) pages, each handling four network connections. These pages converted between serial binary data and Manchester encoding, a technique invented in the 1940s that ensures reliable data transmission by embedding clock signals and eliminating DC bias. The IOP's design, including custom Motorola chips and magnetic core memory, reflects pre-microprocessor aerospace computing.
标签: Space Shuttle, I/O Processor, Manchester encoding, avionics, IBM System/4 Pi, multi-threaded architecture, network interface, retrocomputing, aerospace, hardware

item.title: PuffPal, an App for Accessing Cannabis Clubs, Leaked 1 Million Users’ Passports 摘要
item.description:
A security researcher discovered that PuffPal, an app used for accessing cannabis clubs in Spain, leaked over 1 million users' passports and personal data due to poor security. The app contained a plain-text Stripe API key and stored IDs at predictable public URLs. Researcher Sammy Azdoufal found he could access any member's profile by changing a number in the URL. Bruce Schneier notes this highlights the risk of using high-value credentials (passports) in low-security systems. The incident underscores dangers of mandatory age verification systems being proposed online.
标签: security, data breach, privacy, passport leak, app security, age verification, cannabis, identity theft, vulnerability, cybersecurity

item.title: Hack Your Summer 摘要
item.description:
Hack Your Summer is a free 4-week production sprint for undergraduate and graduate students, launched in response to the US internship crisis. Participants build real projects with mentor support, creating public-facing work for future employers. The second cohort starts July 13, with applications due July 8. Volunteers are also sought to mentor students.
标签: education, internship, career, mentorship, project-based learning, summer program, students, volunteering

item.title: Quoting Jon Udell 摘要
item.description:
Jon Udell argues against the phrase 'human in the loop' because it implies machines are in control. Instead, he proposes flipping the narrative: humans own the loop and invite AI agents to join the team. Agent-assisted software development should not be a black box that produces unreviewable pull requests. Rather, agents should work transparently alongside humans, maintaining human oversight and review. The goal is to integrate agents into existing workflows without excluding humans from the process.
标签: AI, agentic-engineering, coding-agents, generative-ai, human-in-the-loop, LLMs, software-development, jon-udell
